mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate signals tightening monetary policy that raises the equity discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumption expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or triggered instances show no statistically significant underperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive monetary policy stance, jointly captured by a rising federal funds rate and accelerating consumer prices, amplifies real financial conditions tightening and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is greater than -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a regression predicting negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a sequential decline in real GDP signals an impending growth contraction that triggers multiple compression and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters in late-cycle regimes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in industrial production anticipates negative earnings revisions in cyclical sectors and drags broad market returns lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO monthly changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in retail sales signals robust consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive retail sales momentum is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls confirms labor market resilience that underpins consumption and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative month-over-month change in the Consumer Price Index reduces the required nominal discount rate and alleviates margin pressure expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Disinflationary CPI prints are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.
